campbell.title: Campbell City Council — April 21, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to discuss updating the tax rates that businesses pay for their licenses in the city. Shannon Dragman from the finance department presented the proposed changes to these tax rates, and the council publicly discussed and voted on whether to approve the new rates.
keyTopics: Business license tax rates are being increased
Finance department presented the annual rate update
Council deliberated and made a decision on the new rates
Changes will take effect in the new fiscal year

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026, where residents could comment on a proposed new rule or policy. After hearing from the public, the council introduced an ordinance (new law) and skipped the normal first-reading delay so they could vote on it faster at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held for community input on proposed ordinance
Council waived first reading to expedite the approval process
Ordinance scheduled for final vote at next regular council meeting
Specific ordinance topic not detailed in agenda summary

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 21, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 21, 2026, to approve meeting minutes from earlier in the month and review the city's finances for February. The council also authorized several construction and professional service contracts to move forward with various town improvement projects.
keyTopics: Approved previous meeting minutes and closed session records
Reviewed February 2026 financial and investment report
Authorized construction contracts with QLM, Inc. and other vendors
Approved town improvement projects and professional service agreements

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 27, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council reviewed progress reports on several major city initiatives, including how well the city is executing its long-term planning goals, updates on Team San José's work, and what's happening with big events planned for 2026. They also discussed the city's economic development strategy and whether it's staying on track with its annual targets.
keyTopics: Progress on citywide planning projects and development activities
Team San José initiatives and their six-month performance results
2026 major events planning and status updates
Economic strategy goals and whether the city is meeting its targets

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 27, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting on April 27, 2026 that allowed residents to participate both in person and online. The council provided instructions for residents who wanted to speak during the meeting using Zoom or by phone.
keyTopics: Virtual meeting access via Zoom and phone dial-in
How to raise your hand and give public comment
Meeting conducted with hybrid in-person and online participation

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 27, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a special evening meeting on April 27, 2026, to interview candidates for various city boards and commissions. Residents had the opportunity to participate both in person and online to watch the interviews and provide public comment.
keyTopics: Board and commission candidate interviews
Public comment period for residents
In-person and online participation options

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 27, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held an Audit Committee meeting where residents were invited to speak about any city matters for up to three minutes each. The committee approved minutes from a previous January meeting and reviewed how well the city's retirement and health benefit trust investments performed over the first three months of 2026.
keyTopics: Public comment period on city matters
Approval of January 26 meeting minutes
Retirement and health benefits investment performance (Q1 2026)
Section 115 Trust fund report

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 21, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council met to discuss allowing large promotional signs to be displayed on buildings around Levi's Stadium during the 2026 FIFA World Cup events. The council also approved a new labor agreement with the city's firefighters covering 2026-2029. One agenda item was withdrawn by staff and did not proceed.
keyTopics: Temporary supergraphic signs approved for Levi's Stadium during FIFA World Cup 2026
New three-year labor contract finalized with Santa Clara firefighters
One agenda item withdrawn before discussion

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 21, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council held a meeting on April 21, 2026, to make decisions on several important city matters including tax rules, next year's development budget, and user fees. The council also heard a presentation about the county's plan to address homelessness and approved previous meeting records.
keyTopics: Tax Equity and Fiscal Responsibility Act reissuance
FY 2026-27 Development Budget approval
Updates to city user fees and charges
County homelessness prevention plan
Meeting minutes and upcoming agenda items

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 23, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The City Council reviewed a request to reconsider a tree removal permit that was already approved for 751 Waverley Street. Someone is asking the Planning Director to take another look at the decision to allow protected trees to be removed from this property.
keyTopics: Protected tree removal permit at 751 Waverley Street
Request for Director's Review of an already-approved permit
Over-the-counter planning entitlement process
